title: MedPruner: Training-Free Hierarchical Token Pruning for Efficient 3D Medical Image Understanding in Vision-Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2603.11625v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: While specialized Medical Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable success in interpreting 2D and 3D medical modalities, their deployment for 3D volumetric data remains constrained by significant computational inefficiencies. Current architectures typically suffer from massive anatomical redundancy due to the direct concatenation of consecutive 2D slices and lack the flexibility to handle heterogeneous information densities across different slices using fixed pruning ratios. To address these challenges, we propose MedPruner, a training-free and model-agnostic hierarchical token pruning framework specifically designed for efficient 3D medical image understanding. MedPruner introduces a two-stage mechanism: an Inter-slice Anchor-based Filtering module to eliminate slice-level temporal redundancy, followed by a Dynamic Information Nucleus Selection strategy that achieves adaptive token-level compression by quantifyin

Title: MedPruner: Training-Free Hierarchical Token Pruning for Efficient 3D Medical Image Understanding in Vision-Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2603.11625v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: While specialized Medical Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable success in interpreting 2D and 3D medical modalities, their deployment for 3D volumetric data remains constrained by significant computational inefficiencies. To address these challenges, we propose MedPruner, a training-free and model-agnostic hierarchical token pruning framework specifically designed for efficient 3D medical image understanding. MedPruner introduces a two-stage mechanism: an Inter-slice Anchor-based Filtering module to eliminate slice-level temporal redundancy, followed by a Dynamic Information Nucleus Selection strategy that achieves adaptive token-level compression by quantifyin
translated_title: MedPruner：面向视觉语言模型中高效三维医学图像理解的无训练层次化令牌剪枝框架
translated_abstract: arXiv:2603.11625v2 公告类型：替换-交叉 摘要：尽管专门的医学视觉语言模型（VLM）在解释2D和3D医学模态方面取得了显著成功，但其在三维体数据上的部署仍受到显著计算效率低下的制约。当前架构通常因直接拼接连续的2D切片而存在大量解剖冗余，并且缺乏通过固定剪枝比灵活处理不同切片间异质信息密度的能力。为应对这些挑战，我们提出MedPruner，一个专为高效三维医学图像理解设计的无训练且模型无关的层次化令牌剪枝框架。MedPruner引入了一个两阶段机制：首先是基于切片间锚点的过滤模块，用于消除切片级别的时间冗余；接着是动态信息核选择策略，通过量化实现自适应令牌级压缩。
tldr: 本文提出MedPruner，一个无训练、模型无关的层次化令牌剪枝框架，通过两阶段机制（切片间冗余消除与动态信息核选择）大幅提升三维医学图像理解在视觉语言模型中的计算效率。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes MedPruner, a training-free, model-agnostic hierarchical token pruning framework that significantly improves computational efficiency for 3D medical image understanding in VLMs through a two-stage mechanism of inter-slice redundancy elimination and dynamic information nucleus selection.
